# 津軽 (Tsugaru) ### AOJ · v0.2.0 Part of the [Japan Map Pack](https://ahkimn.github.io/subwaybuilder-jp-maps). ## Additional Features - **Building Collision** — A buildings index is included for all non-filtered buildings, each carrying a per-building foundation depth in in-game meters. Depths scale with building height and footprint width and are bounded to the **−10 m** to **−80 m** range: single-family homes and low-rise structures anchor at the floor, while supertall towers with deep bedrock piles (Taipei-101 class) reach the cap. Freestanding towers, masts, chimneys, and other slender non-occupiable structures are detected by shape and floored to the **−10 m** minimum. Foundation depths drive underground tunnel-clearance collision and are also emitted as a visual `foundations` pmtiles layer visible on the in-game map. Heights come from the GlobalBuildingAtlas LoD1 ML-inferred height dataset with the Overture Maps Foundation footprint as the geometric backbone. - **Water Depth Index** — A water depth index derived from J-EGG500 500 m-mesh bathymetry data restricts track placement in open water. Ocean areas use a graduated depth from the bathymetry raster, while inland waterways (rivers, lakes) use a shallower **−5 m** depth — both prevent surface-level track placement in water bodies. - **Ocean Foundations** — A depth-colored ocean floor layer is included for coastal bundles, providing a visual representation of ocean and coastal bathymetry visible on the in-game map. - **Place Name Labels** — Three layers of place name labels are embedded in the map: - _City labels_ — Major designated city names (_seirei shitei toshi_ / 政令指定都市) at the metropolitan scale. - _Municipality labels_ — Names of all cities, towns, and villages (_shikuchōson_ / 市区町村) within the map boundary. - _Neighborhood labels_ — Sub-municipal district names corresponding to hamlet and village subdivisions (_ōaza_ / 大字) from MLIT National Land Numerical Information, with automatic fallback to block-level names (_chōchō_ / 町丁目) where subdivision boundary data is unavailable. ## Methodology This map is generated through a six-phase pipeline that transforms Japanese government census and employment statistics into georeferenced origin/destination demand points. **Point Seeding:** Candidate points are placed within each _chōchō_ (町丁目 — small census area) using population-weighted centroids derived from a 100 m building-footprint mesh. Dense districts are split into multiple points using adaptive soft caps scaled by local density, while sparse rural areas use building footprint data to anchor points at actual settlement locations rather than geometric centers. **Mesh Reconciliation:** Initial point populations are refined by solving a constrained convex optimization that brings each point's mass closer to 250 m residential mesh and 500 m economic mesh ideals, while preserving the National Census (_令和2年国勢調査_) totals for each _chōchō_. Residents and workers are reconciled independently. **Aggregation:** A global scaling factor reconciles the mismatch between boundary-captured employed residents (from the National Census) and boundary-captured economic employees (from the Economic Census, _令和3年経済センサス_), since commuters cross the boundary in both directions. Small below-threshold points are merged into neighbors to maintain a minimum per-point mass. **Commute Assignment:** Commute flows are assigned using a gravity model with distance-decay, augmented by municipality-level origin-destination targets. An iterative calibration loop adjusts municipality-pair multipliers until simulated commute shares converge toward the observed inter-municipal worker flows. Destination capacity constraints prevent over-assignment. **Special Demand Injection:** Non-commuter demand generators — airports, ports, hospitals, schools, universities, military installations, and tourism attractions — are injected as an additive layer. Each facility's demand is estimated from its real-world throughput (annual passengers, bed occupancy, enrollment) and converted to a daily equivalent using facility-specific haircut factors. **Layout Reconciliation:** A final spatial pass resolves point overlaps and ensures map clickability by adjusting regular-point positions while keeping special-demand locations fixed. ## Data Sources All demand modeling is derived from freely available Japanese government statistical datasets and public geospatial resources. ### Census & Demographics - **2020 National Census** (_令和2年国勢調査_ — per-municipality and per-chōchō population, employment, and demographics) — [e-Stat](https://www.e-stat.go.jp/) - **2021 Economic Census** (_令和3年経済センサス_ — per-establishment employment by industry at 500 m mesh and municipality grain) — [e-Stat](https://www.e-stat.go.jp/) ### Geospatial Reference - **100 m Mesh Population** (_簡易100mメッシュ人口_ — GTFS-GIS dasymetric 100 m population product used as the seed density signal) — [GTFS-GIS](https://gtfs-gis.jp/teikyo/) - **Administrative Boundaries & Land Use** (_国土数値情報_ / MLIT National Land Numerical Information — N03 municipal polygons, chōchō boundaries, and L03-b land-use tiles) — [MLIT](https://nlftp.mlit.go.jp/) - **Building Heights** (GlobalBuildingAtlas / GBA LoD1 — ML-inferred per-building height dataset used for the 3D building tiles and the foundation-depth cascade) — [TUM Zhu-XLab on Hugging Face](https://huggingface.co/datasets/zhu-xlab/GBA.LoD1) - **Overture Maps Foundation** (per-building footprint side-channel for the height cascade + Phase E POI auto-geocoding) — [Overture](https://overturemaps.org/) - **Bathymetry** (_海しる_ / MSIL Marine Information Layer + J-EGG500 500 m-mesh ocean depths — coastal + inland waterway depths) — [MSIL](https://www.msil.go.jp/) · [JODC](https://www.jodc.go.jp/) ### Special Demand Statistics - **Airport Passenger Statistics** (_空港管理状況調査_ / MLIT terminal-level annual passengers) — [MLIT Aviation](https://www.mlit.go.jp/koku/15_bf_000185.html) - **Port Passenger Statistics** (_港湾調査_ / MLIT terminal-level passenger throughput) — [MLIT Ports](https://www.mlit.go.jp/k-toukei/kouwan.html) - **University Registry & Enrollment** (_大学ポートレート_ / JASSO institutional metadata and per-faculty student headcount) — [JASSO](https://portraits.niad.ac.jp/) - **School Basic Survey** (_学校基本調査_ / MEXT annual census across primary, secondary, junior college, university, and special-support schools) — [MEXT](https://www.mext.go.jp/) - **Tourism Visitor Statistics** (_観光統計_ / JNTO annual visitor counts for tourism attractions) — [JNTO](https://statistics.jnto.go.jp/) - **Hospital Registry & Bed Statistics** (_医療施設調査_ / MHLW annual medical-facility survey with per-facility bed counts) — [MHLW](https://www.mhlw.go.jp/) - **Military Installations** (JSDF garrison roster — active-duty personnel estimated from unit composition, curated from official unit references and public sources) — [JSDF / 防衛省](https://www.mod.go.jp/) ## License This map data is released under the [GNU General Public License v3.0](https://github.com/ahkimn/subwaybuilder-jp-maps/blob/main/LICENSE). ## Credits Map authored by [Yukina-](https://subwaybuildermodded.com/credits/)
